## prompt-5-draft-bill

*Generated by Process A from `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/draft-bill/prompt-draft-bill.md`, adapted for the single-prompt v4.0 build.*

Write the H. R. 9510 Bill v4.0 draft (scaffold) LaTeX into
`auto-bill-01/draft-bill`, adapting the paper template format of
`cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/final-bill` and using less numbered and lettered
lists. Preserve the look of professional text-based diagrams and tables exactly as
they render in GitHub Markdown. No PNG or JPG images; the only diagrams permitted
are **gray-scale Mermaid** diagrams, reproduced in LaTeX as gray-scale TikZ
figures. Make sure all Markdown effects and tables have a white background
throughout all of the LaTeX files.

The draft is a scaffold: it lays out the full congressional amendment structure
(caption; A BILL; enacting clause; SECTION 1 short title and a clickable,
page-filling table of contents; SEC. 2 findings; SEC. 3 the operative amendment
and new section 515D / 21 U.S.C. 360e-5; SEC. 4 comparative print) and, for the
twelve deliverable appendices (Appendix A through Appendix L, one per deliverable
in `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/final-bill/deliverables`), it provides bracketed
[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS] that name the exact upstream files to process and the
gray-scale Mermaid figure each appendix will carry, rather than the finished prose.
Every appendix must appear in the SECTION 1(b) table of contents. Include the
"Authorship, Notices, and Provenance" block and the References back matter (Rule 3).

The bill must be (a) up to date with today's medical-AI law and its medical and
technology complexities, (b) grounded in current, mass-adopted medical-law
references used meaningfully, (c) relevant to emerging bills only as research
influences (kept out of the operative text and confined to a deliverable
appendix), and (d) streamlined to the structured amendment format.

One commit each for `main.tex`, `usctitle.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`;
one commit per `sections/*.tex` file (each corresponds to `main.tex`). Define
sections by the figures and tables they share. Commit and push in real time; do
not hold commits. For the second-to-last commit, fix all errors across all files.
For the last commit, write the directory README (repository structure, badges,
ASCII-free gray-scale Mermaid diagram, table of contents) and the
`draft-bill-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle. All commits belong to a single pull
request.

Formatting (centralize in `usctitle.sty`): white background everywhere; RaggedRight
body with even interword spacing and no right-margin overflow; modify
`\RaggedRightRightskip` so words are evenly spaced with no large gaps; maximal
widow and orphan penalties so no single line is stranded and no one- or two-word
last line is left behind; avoid single lines separated from a paragraph onto the
next page; correct symbols such as "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only (no en, em,
double, or triple dashes); every table column uses
`>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` (the `L`/`ragcol` helpers) or the
flexible `Y` column, set to `\textwidth`; use `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and
`\hspace` as a senior author would to balance each page. The cover page keeps its
context but closes with a gray-scale Mermaid (TikZ) overview of the v4.0 build
process; condense the House and Senate statements; date June 7, 2026.

Create `auto-bill-01/draft-bill/prompt-draft-bill.md` (this prompt verbatim under
a `## prompt-draft-bill` heading) and `output-draft-bill.md` (the narrative
output). Use only Claude Code Opus 4.8 (1M context) Max. Do not stall, ask
questions, or go into plan mode. The LaTeX must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX.
